title: Fix local:: false error in randomize() with on parameterized class (#6680)
body:
## Summary

This patch fixes `local::` being falsely rejected inside `randomize() with`
when the randomization target is a parameterized class. Fixes #6680.

During the primary linking pass, `m_randSymp` cannot be set for parameterized
classes (because the class is not yet resolved -- that happens in V3Param).
The `local::` validity check only tested `m_randSymp`, missing the case where
the call context is correctly established via `m_randMethodCallp`.

## Reproducer

```systemverilog
class item #(type T);
  rand T value;
endclass

class driver #(type T);
  task run(T val);
    item#(T) it;
    it = new();
    if (!it.randomize() with { value == local::val; })
      $error("fail");
  endtask
endclass
```

On master:
```
%Error: t.sv:10:38: Illegal 'local::' outside 'randomize() with' (IEEE 1800-2023 18.7.1)
   10 |     if (!it.randomize() with { value == local::val; })
      |                                         ^~~~~
```

## Changes

- `src/V3LinkDot.cpp` (`visit(AstClassOrPackageRef*)`): Add `m_randMethodCallp` as alternative indicator in `local::` validity check, allowing `local::` when inside a `randomize()` call on a parameterized class target

## Test

- Added `test_regress/t/t_randomize_local_paramed.{v,py}` -- `local::` with parameterized class in `randomize() with`, single-param and multi-param variants

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
